facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Lio openly backs Rowan under pressure and leaves with him to investigate from outside the ropes; Nessa shifts from cautious gatekeeper to active covert archive ally, keeps the copied transfer note herself, and agrees to trace clerical scars while warning Pell should get any confirmed destination; Pell remains an off-page trusted recipient for urgent updates

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: The South Third struggle is now a live race over both interpretation and jurisdiction: Rowan must verify the new two-return sounding and move information to trusted allies before Talren, Pike, or formal hazard machinery can frame the hidden structure and missing-men case to suit their own claims

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Rowan still lacks money, license, sponsor, and lawful cordon access; Hall Review now directly knows he suspects older pre-district work and deadened resonance; Pike is escalating through private petition, emergency-funding leverage, and on-the-ground contractors; Review is actively pulling South Third packets and civic maintenance records topside

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Get ahead of Pike and Review at South Third, confirm the changed soundings, get critical updates to Pell, and let Nessa trace the vault-transfer trail before official access locks down

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Outside the archive building in the evening street, with Rowan and Lio moving toward South Third while Nessa remains in the archive system to trace records

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Unranked, unlicensed salvage runner; shoulder still in sling and actively hurting, with added head pain; provisional notice and pending deposition unchanged; now more strategically defiant after refusing Talren full disclosure; no longer carrying the copied transfer note because Nessa kept it; newly aware that Pike, Hall Review, and other interests are racing to control both the records and the sounding narrative
